title: Programmable Self
html:
The Mindset

Just do it, feel it, and let yourself get relieved of constantly trusting your system. The point is not to prove the machine works on day one; it is to run it long enough that trust stops being a question. Remember that tangible results take time and patience — the compounding is real, but it is slow, and it rewards the people who stop checking the scoreboard every hour.

The Components

If you treat the mind as an operating system, there are a couple of programmatic MindOS components that map surprisingly cleanly:

  • Calendar is the process scheduler — it decides what runs and when.
  • To-do lists are the wait queues — everything blocked, pending, waiting for its slot.
  • All the mental models: Identities, Principles, Mental Models, Operation Modes/Contexts are just implementation frameworks — the code you write on top of the kernel.

Type of Processes

  • Programs: They should be preemptive with timers, so that no single task can hog the whole day without being interrupted and rescheduled.
  • Reflection: A meta program at the start and end of a cycle. This is the source of MindOS plasticity — the mechanism by which the system rewrites its own rules instead of running the same flawed loop forever.

Qualities

  • Reliability: how instructions are adhered to. In other words, discipline — the quiet quality that decides whether any of the above is more than a diagram.

(Built on an operating-system metaphor I keep returning to in a friend’s garden of thoughts.)

  • Life Running Digital Engine
